description: Grants a watcher-opened devserver window (lib-*) served from the user's own gateway tunnel origin (https://*.devserver.chan.app) the same command reach as its loopback-served twin: the workspace-window app set (window lifecycle for the close-confirm and disconnect overlays, reload/devtools, the zoom trio, save_file_to_downloads, probe_url, platform_os, and all six clipboard commands), the native upload picker (cs upload), native fullscreen and webview zoom, and the system-browser opener. This remote-IPC grant is DELIBERATE: the tunnel origin serves the user's own PAT-backed devserver through the chan gateway, so its content carries the same trust as the loopback serve of the same library -- without these grants the whole command vocabulary dies there as silent ACL denials (dead upload/download/paste, dead chords). The one command a loopback workspace window holds that lib-* windows never get -- on ANY origin -- is read_dropped_paths: the macOS drag pasteboard is system-wide and outlives the drag, so it stays scoped to locally-supervised window kinds via local-drop.json. Self-hosted gateways on other domains are outside this static origin list. serve.rs's origin-aware ACL parity test recomputes this file's reach against the SPA's invoke vocabulary; the shape pin beside it keeps the grant list and origin scope exact.
